article_title: B.C. Reg. 28/2026 – British Columbia Energy Regulator
body:
Regulation: B.C. Reg. 28/2026
Made under: Energy Resource Activities Act
Published in: No. 4 – B.C. Regs. 18 to 28, March 10, 2026
Deposit date: March 10, 2026

B.C. Reg. 28/2026, deposited March 10, 2026, under theENERGY RESOURCE ACTIVITIES ACT[sections 106 to 109, 111 and 112]. Regulation of the Board of the British Columbia Energy Regulator, dated March 5, 2026. The board of the British Columbia Energy Regulator orders that the attached Renewable Energy Projects Regulation is made. — N. KIERNAN,Chair, Board of Directors. RENEWABLE ENERGY PROJECTS REGULATION Part 1 – Interpretation and Application "Act"means theEnergy Resource Activities Act; "local first nation", in relation to a renewable energy project, means a first nation identified for the project in a manner specified by the regulator; "major component", in relation to a renewable energy facility, means (b)	a battery or other energy storage system; "non-exempt reviewable project"means a reviewable project within the meaning of theEnvironmental Assessment Actthat is not specified under section 14 (a) to (d) of theRenewable Energy Projects (Streamlined Permitting) Act; "notice of construction start"means a notice under section 42 (1) (b); "notice of operation start"means a notice under section 43 (1); "project area", in relation to a renewable energy project, means any of the following areas: (a)	an area in which a renewable energy activity is to be carried out, if the project is a level 3 streamlined project; (b)	an area in which another activity that is required for or facilitates a renewable energy activity is to be carried out; "qualified professional", in relation to a matter, means an individual who (a)	is registered with a regulatory body as defined in section 1 (1)[definitions and interpretation]of theProfessional Governance Act, and (b)	through suitable education, experience, accreditation and knowledge, may reasonably be relied on to provide advice within the individual’s area of expertise as it relates to the matter; (2)	Unless a contrary intention appears, words and expressions used in this regulation have the same meaning as in theRenewable Energy Projects (Streamlined Permitting) Act. 2(1)	Subject to this section, this regulation applies in relation to level 2 and level 3 streamlined projects.

minutes_briefs.title: New BC regulation sets rules for renewable energy projects
minutes_briefs.summary: The BC Energy Regulator has created a new regulation (B.C. Reg. 28/2026) that sets out detailed rules for renewable energy projects, including definitions for key terms like 'local first nation', 'major component' (which includes batteries), and 'qualified professional'. This regulation applies to level 2 and level 3 streamlined projects under the Renewable Energy Projects (Streamlined Permitting) Act.
